denominator_note: Denominator is TOTAL project investment (includes IT/servers/power/land), NOT construction-only capex. Construction is typically ~40-55% of total DC project cost, so construction-jobs-per-construction-$ would be roughly 2x these values.

construction_peak_workers_per_musd.note: Meta Richland Parish only source with a clean construction figure + capex.

headline.dc_construction_peak_workers_per_ausd_mu: ~0.2-0.7 peak construction workers per $1M of total investment (central ~0.5)
headline.dc_operations_jobs_per_musd: ~0.05-0.17 permanent jobs per $1M of total investment (central ~0.1)
headline.per_dollar_employment_cliff: ~5x fewer permanent than peak-construction jobs per $ invested (consistent with the per-MW cliff)

comparator_context.minnesota_construction_sector: 3.93 construction jobs per $1M of construction output (Minnesota LBO) — general construction is far more labour-intensive per $ than data-centre construction
comparator_context.interpretation: A dollar invested in a data centre buys far fewer construction jobs than a dollar of general construction spending, because DC project cost is dominated by imported equipment (servers, power, cooling), not on-site labour.
